title: Memory, Storage, RAID, and Removable Media · Support Retrieval
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs memory, storage, raid, and removable media from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for memory and storage integrity decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Compare RAM generation, form factor, capacity, channels, speed, timing, voltage, ECC, registered status, compatibility, and population rules.
Distinguish capacity pressure, application faults, thermal instability, compatibility, contact, and hardware errors using repeatable evidence.
Compare magnetic, flash, optical, removable, SATA, SAS, NVMe, form factor, interface, endurance, latency, throughput, and workload fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Ram Properties
steps.instruction:
Order the Ram Properties CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

steps.why: Retrieving Storage Health Custody through the concrete memory and storage integrity decision record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Record what required effort · Review Storage Health Custody, keep Storage Health Custody connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Record what required effort · Review Storage Health Custody, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Storage Health Custody precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Record what required effort · Review Storage Health Custody, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Crashes, storage pressure, a degraded array, and a healthy-looking drive blur capacity, hardware, filesystem, redundancy, backup, and custody boundaries. The review variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

For CompTIA A+ support Record what required effort · Review Storage Health Custody, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A drive health percentage is a complete diagnosis and exact lifetime forecast.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
